Our College courses

School of Business

Build strong foundations in entrepreneurship, finance, marketing, and business operations. Ideal for anyone looking to run a business or support a company or manage finances.

Learn more
School of English

Enhance your fluency, confidence, and professional language skills. This course is perfect for job interviews, customer service roles, presentations, and communication.

Learn more
School of Hairdressing

Blend creativity with technical skill. Learn styling, cutting, color treatment, skin care, and salon management in fully equipped beauty labs. Graduate ready to work/run a business. 

Learn more
School of Hospitality

Turn your passion for service into a career. Gain practical experience in food service, customer care, housekeeping, and event coordination. You acquire key skills in Kenya’s hospitality industry.

Learn more
School of Electrical Engineering

From wiring systems to industrial installations, this program teaches you the fundamentals of electricity and electronics through real-world projects

Learn more
School of ICT

Master the backbone of today’s digital world. Learn computer systems, networking, cybersecurity, and software skills. We open doors to jobs in tech and beyond.

Learn more
Start building your dream today!
Apply online, book a visit, or talk to an advisor today.
Apply Now
Online classes available for selected programs.”
Study from home, on your schedule — with full support from our tutors.
Get Started

How to Apply

Start your journey at Global College in 3 simple steps.

01

Choose Your Program

Explore our courses and select the program that matches your career goals.

02

Submit Your Application

Complete the application form with your personal and academic details.

03

Start Your Learning Journey

Once approved, enroll and begin building the skills for your future career.

At Global Technical College, we believe in the power of practical education to transform lives. Since our founding, we’ve helped thousands of students gain real-world skills.

Ronald

Principal
